mainEntity.name: What phases of data center construction do you handle?

acceptedAnswer.text: We handle the full lifecycle: site preparation, foundation and concrete, structural steel, roofing, mechanical/electrical/plumbing (MEP), fire suppression, interior buildout, and commissioning support.

mainEntity.name: Do you work as a prime contractor or subcontractor?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both. We serve as prime GC on smaller builds and as a specialty subcontractor on major projects like those at the Big Cedar Industrial Park.

mainEntity.name: What makes you different from other GCs?

acceptedAnswer.text: We own our freight fleet. Materials arrive on our trucks, on our schedule. No third-party logistics delays. The owner, Mohamed Soliman, personally supervises every project.

mainEntity.name: How does integrated logistics help my data center project?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most construction delays come from materials not arriving on time. Because we own our trucks, we control the delivery schedule. Your steel arrives when your crew is ready to install — not when a third-party carrier gets around to it.

mainEntity.name: Can you handle oversized or heavy equipment?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. We transport generators, transformers, HVAC chillers, and other heavy equipment that data centers require. Our drivers are trained for construction site deliveries with tight access and staging requirements.

mainEntity.name: Do you deliver for other contractors on the same project?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. If you're a subcontractor on a data center build and need reliable local freight, we provide materials delivery services independently of our GC work.

mainEntity.name: What electrical work do you perform for data centers?

acceptedAnswer.text: We handle transformer pad construction, generator foundation and installation support, conduit and cable tray systems, power distribution unit placement, and coordination with electrical subcontractors like Helix Electric.

mainEntity.name: Do you work with the major electrical contractors in Iowa?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. We coordinate closely with firms like Helix Electric and other specialty electrical contractors active on the QTS and Google campuses in the Cedar Rapids corridor.

mainEntity.name: Do you handle data center cooling systems?

acceptedAnswer.text: We provide construction support for chiller plants, cooling towers, air handler units, and the structural work needed for hot/cold aisle containment systems.

mainEntity.name: Can you work with water-free cooling systems?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. QTS's Cedar Rapids campus uses water-free cooling technology. We have experience with the structural and mechanical prep work these next-generation systems require.

mainEntity.name: What site prep work do data centers need?

acceptedAnswer.text: Data centers require heavy-duty foundations to support server racks, generators, and cooling equipment. We handle excavation, soil stabilization, foundation pours, raised access flooring prep, and loading dock construction.
